sourcePrompt: An OV-10 Bronco forward-air-control aircraft, about thirteen meters long with its strangest feature in plan view: two slim booms run back from the wing-mounted turboprop engines to a single high tailplane bridging them, framing open air behind the short central pod. That pod carries a huge greenhouse canopy up front and sponson stubs below hung with rocket pods. Parked on tall tricycle gear. Olive-drab over gray, insect-like and unmistakable.
